title: Using the Product
description: Learn how to use AuditMate MFG Revision Manager
content: Learn how to use AuditMate MFG Revision Manager   Using the Product Learn how to use AuditMate MFG Revision Manager for managing your CNC programs through the complete lifecycle.  Workflow Steps The Workflow Steps outline the structured process AuditMate uses to move a part from design to production. Each step ensures proper planning, authorization, and execution: | Step | Description | ||| | Part Identification | Define the physical component to be manufactured. | | Operation Creation | Set up manufacturing operations for the identified part. | | Machine Association | Link each operation to the appropriate machines. | | Program Import | Import CNC programs associated with each operation. | | Authorization | Approve the part and operations for production to ensure quality control. | | Deployment | Send the approved operations and programs to the machines for execution. |  Core Concepts The Core Concepts are the building blocks of AuditMate, representing the entities and relationships that drive the manufacturing workflow: | Concept | Description | ||| | Parts | Physical components with a unique ID, revision, and linked operations. | | Operations | Operations define specific manufacturing activities performed on parts. | | Machines | CNC equipment used to execute operations and programs. | | Programs | CNC instructions that guide machines during production. |       Next Steps After reviewing the workflow, proceed to Part Identification to define the parts for production.  Using the Product Workflow Steps Core Concepts Next Steps

title: Parts & Operations
description: Understand how Parts and Operations work together to manage CNC programs in AuditMate.
content: Understand how Parts and Operations work together to manage CNC programs in AuditMate.   Parts & Operations  Parts and Operations form the core structure of AuditMate MFG Revision Manager. Together, they define how CNC programs are organized, reviewed, approved, and executed throughout their lifecycle.  This structure ensures that:  CNC programs are grouped logically  Revisions are tracked and auditable  Only authorized programs reach production machines  Changes are traceable at every stage  A Part represents the job or item being manufactured, while Operations represent the individual machining steps performed on that Part.  Explore the concepts that drive the workflow:  Parts  Operations  Parts & Operations

title: Machines
description: Learn how to view and manage all machines within AuditMate.
content: Learn how to view and manage all machines within AuditMate.   Machines The Machines page serves as the central library for all machine resources in AuditMate.   It organizes equipment into Machine Types and the specific machines that belong to each type.   This structure helps ensure accurate tracking, reporting, and maintenance of your organization's equipment.      Access: Top Menu → Machines  Next Step After reviewing Machines, proceed to Machine Types to define categories for your machines.  Machines Next Step

title: Getting Started
description: Welcome to AuditMate MFG Revision Manager documentation
content: Welcome to AuditMate MFG Revision Manager documentation   Getting Started Welcome to the AuditMate MFG Revision Manager documentation. This guide will help you get up and running quickly.  Overview AuditMate MFG Revision Manager is a webbased platform designed to streamline the way manufacturers and operators manage CNC programs and related documents.  What You'll Learn  How to install and configure AuditMate  Basic concepts and terminology  Your first steps with the platform  Next Steps Continue to the Installation Guide to begin setting up your system.  Getting Started Overview What You'll Learn Next Steps

title: Contact Information
description: Get in touch with Nexas America support
content: Get in touch with Nexas America support   Contact Information Need help? Get in touch with Nexas America support team.  Technical Support For technical assistance with AuditMate MFG Revision Manager:  Email: support@nexasamerica.com  Phone: Contact your account manager  Hours: Monday  Friday, 8:00 AM  5:00 PM EST  Sales Inquiries Interested in AuditMate for your facility?  Email: sales@nexasamerica.com  Website: www.nexasamerica.com  Documentation This knowledge base is regularly updated. If you find any issues or have suggestions for improvement, please contact our support team.  Emergency Support For critical production issues: 1. Contact your dedicated support representative 2. Provide your facility ID and description of the issue 3. Our team will respond according to your service level agreement  Training Nexas America offers comprehensive training programs:  Onsite training sessions  Remote training webinars  Custom training materials  Ongoing support and refresher courses Contact your account manager to schedule training for your team.  Contact Information Technical Support Sales Inquiries Documentation Emergency Support Training

title: Archive
description: Version control, backup, and historical records
content: Version control, backup, and historical records   Archive The archive system preserves the complete version history of all CNC programs and related documents, supports a structured backup strategy, and provides a revisit function for restoring or reviewing previous versions.      Access: Top Menu → Archive  Features  Automatic version tracking  Complete revision history  Secure backup storage  Easy retrieval of past versions  Accessing Archived Files 1. Navigate to the Archive section 2. Search by part name, part revision, operation name, operation revision or archived date 3. Select the version you need 4. Preview or restore as needed  Archive Features Accessing Archived Files

title: Sending to Machine
description: Deploy programs to production machines
content: Deploy programs to production machines   Sending to Machine Deploy authorized CNC programs to production machines.  Prerequisite Before sending CNC programs to a machine, they must be tested and correctly tagged in the system. To update the program’s download status:  Click the Untested status.  The Authorize Download Status Change form will open.  Toggle the status to Authorize.  Click Save. Once updated to Tested, the program becomes eligible for Sending to Machine. ⚠️ Note: Control who can authorize downloads in: Settings  Global Settings  Download Status Authorization <br  Send to Machine |  |  | ||| |  Click the Send button for the corresponding machine and head. <br  The Send to Machine form will open. <br  Select the programs you want to send by checking the boxes next to each program. <br  Click Send to Machine to begin the transfer. <br<br The system will process the request and handle delivery based on the configured communication method for that machine. |  |  Transfer Methods AuditMate supports multiple transfer protocols:  Network Transfer – Direct IPbased communication to compatible machines.    DNC (Direct Numerical Control) – Traditional RS232 or serialbased transfer.    File Share – Sending programs to a monitored network folder for machine retrieval.    Troubleshooting  Connection Failed  Verify machine is powered on  Check network connectivity  Confirm IP address and port settings  Transfer Timeout  Check file size limits  Verify machine storage availability  Review communication settings  Program Not Found on Machine  Confirm transfer completed successfully  Check destination folder on machine  Verify file naming conventions  Sending to Machine Prerequisite Send to Machine Transfer Methods Troubleshooting Connection Failed Transfer Timeout Program Not Found on Machine

title: Part Identification
description: Define and configure parts to be manufactured in AuditMate, including properties such as name, revision, and authorization type.
content: Define and configure parts to be manufactured in AuditMate, including properties such as name, revision, and authorization type.   Part Identification A Part represents a physical component to be manufactured. In AuditMate, each part acts as a container for related operations and CNC programs, serving as the foundation of your manufacturing workflow.    Access: Top Menu → Parts and Operations, Left Menu → Parts. <br The Left Panel displays all existing parts created in the system.  Create a New Part |  |  | ||| | You can create and define a new part to be manufactured within your facility. <br<br  Click  Create in the Left Panel. <br  Fill in the required details in the Create Part form. <br  Click Save to add the part to the system. <br<br Once created, the part will be listed and available for further setup such as adding operations or CNC programs. |  |  Part Properties | Property | Description | ||| | Part Name | A unique and descriptive identifier for the part. Use consistent naming conventions such as part numbers, drawing numbers, or internal codes. | | Part Revision | Version control identifier. Formats are configured in Settings → Global Settings → Part Revision Format.  | | Part Authorization Type | Determines who can approve parts for production. Configure these under Settings → Global Settings → Part Authorization Types to align with quality control workflows. | | Part Description | Optional field for providing additional context or details about the part. |  Manage Parts Parts Menu    Displayed on the Left Panel and becomes available when a part is selected. | Action | Description | ||| | Rev | Update the part’s revision number to reflect design, specification, or documentation changes. | | Edit | Modify the part’s details. | | Copy | Create a duplicate of the part, including its associated operations, for faster setup of similar parts. | | Archive | Move the part to the archive when it is no longer active but must remain in the system for historical reference. | | Hold | Temporarily restrict the part from being used in operations or production until issues are resolved or approval is granted. | | Release | Available when a part is on hold; restores the part to active status so it can be used again. |  Best Practices  Use consistent naming and revision conventions    Include meaningful descriptions for clarity    Set appropriate authorization types based on part importance    Review existing parts before creating new ones to avoid duplicates    Next Step After identifying parts, proceed to Operation Creation to define the manufacturing steps associated with each part.  Part Identification Create a New Part Part Properties Manage Parts Best Practices Next Step

title: Operation Management
description: Create and manage manufacturing operations for your parts
content: Create and manage manufacturing operations for your parts   Operation Management Operations in AuditMate represent specific manufacturing activities performed on a part. They organize the workflow and link parts to machines.    Access: Top Menu → Parts and Operations, select a part from the list, then go to Operations via the Left Menu or Center Menu. The Left Panel shows all operations for the selected part.  Create an Operation |  |  | ||| |  Select a Part from the list. <br  Click  Create in the Left Panel. <br  Fill in the required details in the Create Operation form. <br  Click Save to add the operation to the system. <br<br Once created, the operation will appear in the Operations list for the selected part. |  | ⚠️ Note: Parts can have multiple operations to represent multistep manufacturing processes.  Manage Operations  Operations Menu    Displayed on the Left Panel and becomes available when an operation is selected. | Action | Description | ||| | Rev | Update the operation’s revision number to track process or setup changes. | | Edit | Modify existing operation details. | | Copy | Duplicate an operation within the same part, including its associated machines and CNC programs, for quicker setup. | | Archive | Move an operation to the archive when it’s no longer active but needs to be retained for reference. | | Hold | Temporarily suspend an operation to prevent it from being used or processed until further notice. | | Release | Available when an operation is on hold; releases the operation from hold status, making it active again. |  Next Step After managing operations, proceed to Machine Association to link operations to machines.  Operation Management Create an Operation Manage Operations  Next Step

title: Machine Association
description: Link operations to machines
content: Link operations to machines   Machine Association Link operations to the machines that will execute them.    Access: Top Menu → Parts and Operations, select a part from the list, then go to Operations via the Left Menu or Center Menu, select the operation, then click on Machines from the Left Menu or Machine Types from the Center Menu. The Left Panel shows all machine types configured for the facility.  Associating a Machine |  | || |  In the Machine Types list, check the box next to the machines you want to associate with the operation. <br<br Once saved, the selected machines will be linked to the operation and can be used for CNC program setup. | |  | || | ⚠️ Note:  Prerequisites <br Part must be created <br  Operation must be defined <br  Machine types and Machines must be configured in the system |  Next Step After associating machines with your operation, proceed to Import CNC Programs to link the necessary programs for manufacturing.  Machine Association Associating a Machine Next Step

title: CNC Program Import
description: Import CNC programs for version control
content: Import CNC programs for version control   CNC Program Import Import CNC programs into AuditMate for version control and deployment.  Before Importing CNC programs are created using external CAM software (like Mastercam, Fusion 360, or similar). AuditMate manages these programs but does not create them.    Access: Top Menu → Parts and Operations, select a part from the list, then go to Operations via the Left Menu or Center Menu. Select the operation, click Machines (Left Menu) or Machine Types (Center Menu), and finally navigate to CNC Programs from the Left or Center Menu.  Import CNC Programs |  | || |  With a machine associated to your operation, click CNC Programs from the menu. <br  For multihead machines, select the correct head number before importing. <br  Import program files using either of the following methods: <br    Drag and Drop: Drag program files from your file explorer and drop them into either the MAIN or SUB panels. <br    Browse for Files: Click Click to Browse, select one or more program files, and click Open. <br<br Once imported, the programs will appear in the CNC Programs list for the selected operation. |   Main vs. Sub Programs  Main Programs The primary CNC program that runs on the machine. Typically contains:  Tool calls  Machining operations  Program flow control  Sub Programs Reusable routines called by main programs:  Common patterns  Subroutines  Macros  After Import Once imported, programs are:  Stored with version control  Ready for review and authorization  Available for editing if needed  Next Step After importing CNC programs, proceed to the Authorization Process to approve programs for production.  CNC Program Import Before Importing Import CNC Programs Main vs. Sub Programs Main Programs Sub Programs After Import Next Step

title: Authorization Process
description: Approve programs for production use
content: Approve programs for production use   Authorization Process Approve parts, operations, and CNC programs for production use.  Who Can Authorize? Authorization permissions are based on:  User role assignments (Configure these in Admin  Users)  Authorization type settings (Configure these in Settings  Global Settings  Part Authorization Types.)  Authorization Process |  |  | ||| |  Log in using an account with authorization permissions. <br  Navigate through: Part → Operation → Machine Type → CNC Programs. <br  Review the program details, including version, revision, and any alerts. <br  Click the Authorization icon in the rightmost column. <br  In the Authorize Program Status Change form, toggle to Authorize, and click Save. |  |  Authorization States  Development – The program is still being created, edited, or validated. Not yet approved for production use.    Production – The program has been reviewed and authorized. Ready for use on the shop floor.  Next Step After authorizing CNC programs, proceed to Sending to a Machine to transfer programs to machines for execution.  Authorization Process Who Can Authorize? Authorization Process Authorization States Next Step

title: Parts
description: Learn how Parts are used to organize CNC programs and production workflows.
content: Learn how Parts are used to organize CNC programs and production workflows.   Parts  A Part represents the manufactured item, job, or work order being produced. It serves as the primary container for all related CNC programs, revisions, and production records.     Access: Top Menu → Parts and Operations, Left Menu → Parts. <br The Left Panel displays all existing parts created in the system.   Purpose of Parts  Using Parts allows you to:  Group all CNC programs related to a specific job  Maintain a clear and complete revision history  Track progress through defined workflow stages  Centralize approvals and audit information  Each Part acts as the toplevel reference point for production, ensuring all associated Operations are properly controlled.   Parts in the Workflow  Parts move through structured workflow stages to ensure programs are reviewed and authorized before production use. Status changes at the Part level provide visibility into overall readiness for manufacturing.  A Part may contain one or more Operations, each with its own CNC program and revision history.  Parts Purpose of Parts Parts in the Workflow

title: Operations
description: Learn how Operations define machining steps and manage CNC programs at a granular level.
content: Learn how Operations define machining steps and manage CNC programs at a granular level.   Operations  An Operation represents a specific machining step performed on a Part. Each Operation is associated with at least one CNC program and defines how that program is executed on a machine.     Access: Top Menu → Parts and Operations, select a part from the list, then go to Operations via the Left Menu or Center Menu. The Left Panel shows all operations for the selected part.   Purpose of Operations  Operations allow you to:  Manage CNC programs at a detailed level  Assign programs to specific machines  Track revisions and approvals per machining step  Control execution in production environments  This level of control ensures accuracy, accountability, and traceability for every machining process.   Operations in the Workflow  Each Operation progresses through the workflow independently while remaining linked to its parent Part. Changes, approvals, and revisions are recorded at the Operation level, creating a complete audit trail for every CNC program.  Operations Purpose of Operations Operations in the Workflow

title: Machine Types
description: Define and categorize your CNC machines
content: Define and categorize your CNC machines   Machine Types ⚠️ Note: Only users with permission to Configure Machines and Machine Types can perform the following task.    Add Machine Type |  |  | ||| | You can create a new machine type to categorize your machines.  <br<br Access the Machines page by clicking Machines in the Top Menu. <br  Click Create, then select Machine Type from the dropdown. This will open the Create Machine Type form. <br  Fill in the required details. <br  Click Save to add the machine type to the system. <br<br Once saved, the new machine type will appear in the Machine Types list. |  |  Edit or Update a Machine Type |  | || | You can modify an existing machine type’s information, such as its name or description. <br<br Click on the corresponding Edit icon of the machine type you want to update. <br  Update the necessary details. <br  Click Save to apply the changes. <br<br Once saved, the updated machine type will reflect immediately in the Machine Types list. <br<br ⚠️ Note: Editing the Machine Head Count is disabled when there are existing Machines associated with the Machine Type. |  Delete a Machine Type |  | || | You can remove a machine type from the system when it is no longer needed. <br<br Click the corresponding Delete icon of the machine type you want to delete. <br  Confirm the action when prompted. <br<br ⚠️ Note: Deleting a Machine Type is permanent and also removes all associated machines. |  Next Step After managing machine types, proceed to Adding Machines to assign individual machines to the machine types.  Machine Types Add Machine Type Edit or Update a Machine Type Delete a Machine Type Next Step

title: Machine Configuration
description: Learn how to configure machine settings, including DNC setup, scanning folders, and communication parameters.
content: Learn how to configure machine settings, including DNC setup, scanning folders, and communication parameters.   Machine Configuration The Machine Configuration is defined within the Create Machine form.   These settings determine how each CNC machine communicates with AuditMate, handles CNC program transfers, and scans for modifications. |  |  | ||| | Create Machine Form (SingleHead) | Create Machine Form (MultiHead) |  Configuration Details | Field / Section | Description | ||| | Machine Name | Unique name that identifies the machine within the system. | | Machine Description | Short description of the machine (e.g., model or purpose). | | DNC Settings | Defines how AuditMate communicates with the machine for sending CNC programs. | | → Workplace ID | Specifies the workstation or network identifier associated with the machine. | | → DNC Type | Determines the communication method for program transfer. <brOptions: <ul<liSend SMB – Transfers via shared folder (SMB protocol).</li<liSend – Sends programs through direct path.</li<liSend SMB to Active Programs Folder – Sends to designated shared folder for active programs.</li</ul | | Program Modifications Scanning | Enables automatic detection of CNC program changes. | | → Scanned Folder | Folder monitored for new or modified CNC programs. | | → DNC Incoming Folder | Directory where scanned or updated programs are received and stored. | | FOCAS Machine Settings | Applies when the machine uses FOCAS (Fanuc Open CNC API) for communication. | | → Fanuc CNC Address | IP address of the Fanuc CNC controller. | | → Fanuc CNC Port | Port number used for communication. | | DNC Type – Specific Options | Additional fields depending on the selected DNC Type. | | → Send SMB | Option to Delete last sent folder during send, clearing previous files before new transfer. | | → Send |  Specify paths for each machine head.<br Active Program Range: Defaults to 0–6999.<br Option to Enable program range deletion to remove programs outside the set range. | | → Send SMB to Active Programs Folder | Requires specifying a Target Directory for storing active programs. | ⚠️ Note: Ensure all network addresses, folder paths, and communication ports are correctly configured to prevent transfer or connectivity errors.  Next Step Proceed to Using the Product to learn about the system’s workflow steps and core concepts, including how parts, operations, and machines interact in the production process.  Machine Configuration Configuration Details Next Step

title: Adding Machines
description: Learn how to add new machines under a Machine Type in AuditMate.
content: Learn how to add new machines under a Machine Type in AuditMate.   Adding Machines There are two ways to add a machine under a Machine Type:  Option 1: Using the Create Button |  |  | ||| | 1. On the Machines page, click the Create button and select Machine from the dropdown. <br 2. The Choose Machine Type form will appear, displaying all machine types available in your facility/facilities. <br 3. Select the machine type where the new machine will be created, then click Next. <br 4. The Create Machine form will appear. |  |  Option 2: Using the  Add Machine Button |  |  | ||| | 1. Expand the machine type where the new machine will be created. <br 2. Click the  Add Machine button. <br 3. The Create Machine form will appear. |  |  Create Machine Form Depending on the machine head count of the selected Machine Type, the Create Machine form that will be shown will vary. <br<br |  |  | ||| | Create Machine Form (SingleHead) | Create Machine Form (MultiHead) |  Fill out the machine details <br   Click Save to create the machine. <br<br  ⚠️Note: Any new machine added to a Machine Type already linked to existing operation(s) will be automatically associated with those operations.  Next Step For a detailed explanation of the machine configuration and setup options, proceed to Machine Configuration.  Adding Machines Option 1: Using the Create Button Option 2: Using the + Add Machine Button Create Machine Form Next Step

title: User Roles
description: Default user roles and permissions in AuditMate
content: Default user roles and permissions in AuditMate   User Roles  Admins can add new roles to the system and manage the permissions assigned to each role. This allows them to control which users can access specific features, perform actions, or view information within the system, ensuring proper access control and security.     Access: Top Menu → Admin, Left Menu → Permissions    Add Role: Click  Add New Role and enter the role name.    Edit Permissions: Check or uncheck boxes to grant or remove permissions.    Save Changes: Click the Save button to apply updates.    Default User Roles and Permissions  Upon installation, default user roles and their permissions are automatically created. This table lists all permissions associated with each role.  | Permission | Administrator | CNC Programming | Engineering | Quality | Setup Tech | View Only | |||||||| | Manage Users/Assign User title: Settings Configuration
description: Learn how to customize AuditMate MFG to your needs by adjusting system settings, managing authorizations, and setting user preferences.
content: Learn how to customize AuditMate MFG to your needs by adjusting system settings, managing authorizations, and setting user preferences. Settings Configuration The Settings menu is accessible from the Top Menu and provides configuration options for both systemwide behavior and individual user preferences. It is divided into two categories: Global Settings and User Settings. GLOBAL SETTINGS | Setting Item | Description | Image | |||| | Part Authorization Types | Define which user roles are authorized to approve Parts, Operations, Machines, and CNC Programs before moving to Production status.

title: Quick Start
description: Get up and running with AuditMate in minutes
content: Get up and running with AuditMate in minutes   Quick Start Get up and running with AuditMate in minutes.  First Login 1. Open your browser and navigate to the AuditMate URL 2. Enter your credentials 3. Select your facility from the facility selection screen  Your First Part Follow these steps to create your first part and CNC program:  Step 1: Create a Part 1. Click Create in the Parts panel 2. Enter part name and revision 3. Select authorization type 4. Save the part  Step 2: Add an Operation 1. Select your newly created part 2. Navigate to the Operations section 3. Click Create to add an operation 4. Enter operation details  Step 3: Associate a Machine 1. With your operation selected, go to Machine Types 2. Check the box next to the machine you want to use 3. The machine is now linked to your operation  Step 4: Import Programs 1. Navigate to CNC Programs 2. Drag and drop your program files 3. Review the imported programs  Step 5: Authorize 1. Click the authorization icon 2. Review the program details 3. Toggle to Authorize and save You're now ready to send programs to your machines!  Quick Start First Login Your First Part Step 1: Create a Part Step 2: Add an Operation Step 3: Associate a Machine Step 4: Import Programs Step 5: Authorize

title: Manage Users
description: Learn how to create, configure, and manage user accounts in AuditMate, including how to add user, assign roles, and set permissions.
content: Learn how to create, configure, and manage user accounts in AuditMate, including how to add user, assign roles, and set permissions.   Manage Users ⚠️Note: Only users with Manage Users / Assign User Roles and Permissions can perform the following task.   For more information about user roles and permissions, see Manage User Roles.      Access: Top Menu → Admin, Left Menu → Users    Add User |  |  | ||| | You can create a new user account and assign appropriate roles and permissions to control access within the system.  <br<br Click Create to open the Create User form.  <br  Fill in the required user details such as Name, Primary Facility, Email, and other relevant information. <br  Assign the appropriate User Role to define access permissions. <br  Click Save to add the new user to the system. <br<br Once saved, the user will appear in the Users list and can log in based on the assigned role and permissions.|  |  Edit or Update a User |  | || | You can modify an existing user’s information, such as role, facility, or contact details. <br<br  Click on the corresponding Edit icon of the user you want to update. <br  Update the necessary details (e.g., name, role, email, or facility). <br  Click Save to apply the changes. <br<br Once saved, the updated user information will reflect immediately in the Users list.|  Delete a User |  | || | You can remove a user from the system when they no longer require access. <br<br Click the corresponding Delete icon of the user you want to delete. <br  Confirm the action when prompted. <br<br Deleting a user is permanent and cannot be undone. Ensure that the user no longer needs system access before proceeding.|  Next Step After managing users, proceed to Manage Machines to configure and organize machine resources within your facilities. Manage Users Add User Edit or Update a User Delete a User Next Step

title: Manage Facilities
description: Learn how to create, edit, and manage facilities in AuditMate.
content: Learn how to create, edit, and manage facilities in AuditMate.   Manage Facilities  A Facility represents a physical location, branch, or operational site within your organization that uses AuditMate.   Each facility may have its own users, machine types, and machines associated with it, allowing AuditMate to organize and report data accurately across different locations.        Access: Top Menu → Admin, Left Menu → Facilities     Add a Facility  |  |  | ||| | You can create a new facility to organize and manage operations within AuditMate.  <br<br Click Create to open the Create Facility form.  <br  Fill in required details. <br  Click Save to add the facility to the system. <br<br Once saved, the new facility will appear in the Facilities list. |  |    Edit or Update a Facility  |  | || | To modify an existing facility’s details: <br<br Locate the facility in the Facilities list. <br  Click on the corresponding Edit icon of the facility you want to update. <br  Update any necessary details. <br  Click Save to apply changes. <br<br Once saved, the updated facility information will reflect immediately in the Facilities list. |     Delete a Facility  |  | || | You can remove a facility from the system when it is no longer needed. <br<br Click on the corresponding Delete icon of the facility you want to delete. <br  Confirm the deletion when prompted. <br<br ⚠️ Note: Deleting a facility is permanent and cannot be undone. Ensure that no users or records are actively linked to the facility before proceeding. |     Next Step  Now that you’ve set up your facility, the next step is to define user roles for your team.   Manage Facilities Add a Facility Edit or Update a Facility Delete a Facility Next Step

title: Main Screen Overview
description: Get up and running with AuditMate in minutes
content: Get up and running with AuditMate in minutes   Main Screen Overview The main screen is your primary workspace. Understanding its layout helps you navigate the system | Section | Description | ||| | Top Menu | Provides access to core functions, navigation options, and system settings| | Left Menu | Displays contextspecific options for the selected topmenu item| | Left Panel | Displays the list of created entities for the selected entity <br(all Parts, Operations for a Part, Machines for an Operation, or CNC Programs for a Machine)| | Center Menu | Provides quick navigation across process entities <br(Part, Operation, Machine, CNC Program)| | Center Panel | Displays the detailed information of the process entity selected|  Next Step Once you are familiar with the Main Screen, continue to: Settings Configuration Main Screen Overview Next Step

title: Introduction
description: Introduction to AuditMate MFG Revision Manager
content: Introduction to AuditMate MFG Revision Manager   Introduction Welcome to the AuditMate MFG Revision Manager, a webbased platform designed to streamline the way manufacturers and operators manage CNC programs and related documents.  System Overview The system provides a centralized hub where users can:  Store CNC programs securely  Organize documents with version control  Preview programs before deployment  Transfer files to machines  Manage user permissions and access  Key Benefits  Version Control Maintain complete control of the CNC program lifecycle with automatic versioning and change tracking.  Data Security Ensure compliance with company standards through document restrictions and user permission management.  Error Reduction Advanced validation and approval workflows help reduce errors and improve quality.  Complete Traceability Every program, file, and communication is logged for compliance and audit purposes.  Built for Manufacturing AuditMate integrates seamlessly with your existing manufacturing processes, supporting both daytoday operations and longterm process reliability.  Introduction System Overview Key Benefits Version Control Data Security Error Reduction Complete Traceability Built for Manufacturing

title: Installation Guide
description: Step-by-step installation instructions
content: Step-by-step installation instructions   Installation Guide This guide will walk you through the installation process for AuditMate MFG Revision Manager.  System Requirements Before installing, ensure your system meets the following requirements:  Operating System: Windows Server 2016 or later  Database: PostgreSQL 12 or later  Browser: Chrome, Firefox, or Edge (latest versions)  Network: Local network access for machine communication  Installation Steps  1. Download the Installer Contact Nexas America to receive your installation package and license key.  2. Run the Setup Wizard Execute the installer and follow the onscreen prompts: `bash  Example installation command setup.exe /install /configconfig.xml `  3. Configure Database Connection During installation, you'll be prompted to configure your database connection:  Host: Your PostgreSQL server address  Port: Default 5432  Database Name: auditmateproduction  Credentials: Database username and password  4. Set Up Admin Account Create your first administrator account with:  Username  Password  Email address  5. Verify Installation After installation completes, verify by accessing: ` http://localhost:3003/login ` Default credentials:  Username: AuditmateAdmin  Password: Testingpw24  Security Note: Change the default password immediately after first login.  PostInstallation After successful installation: 1. Configure facility settings 2. Add machine types 3. Set up user accounts 4. Configure authorization workflows  Troubleshooting  Database Connection Issues If you cannot connect to the database: 1. Verify PostgreSQL service is running 2. Check firewall settings 3. Confirm credentials are correct  Port Conflicts If port 3003 is already in use, modify the configuration in config.xml.  Next Steps Explore the Main Screen Overview to understand the system’s interface. Installation Guide System Requirements Installation Steps 1. Download the Installer 2. Run the Setup Wizard Example installation command 3. Configure Database Connection 4. Set Up Admin Account 5. Verify Installation Post-Installation Troubleshooting Database Connection Issues Port Conflicts Next Steps

title: Getting Started
description: Welcome to AuditMate MFG Revision Manager documentation
content: Welcome to AuditMate MFG Revision Manager documentation   Getting Started Welcome to the AuditMate MFG Revision Manager documentation. This guide will help you get up and running quickly.  Overview AuditMate MFG Revision Manager is a webbased platform designed to streamline the way manufacturers and operators manage CNC programs and related documents.  What You'll Learn  How to install and configure AuditMate  Basic concepts and terminology  Your first steps with the platform  Next Steps Continue to the Installation Guide to begin setting up your system.  Getting Started Overview What You'll Learn Next Steps

title: Revisit
description: Review all part details before archiving, including linked operations, metadata, and validation information.
content: Review all part details before archiving, including linked operations, metadata, and validation information.   Revisit  ⚠️ Note: Only users with permission to View Archive / Revisit can access the Revisit page.     Access: Top Menu → Archive, Left Menu → Revisit   Overview  |  | || | The Revisit page allows users to view a part and its details as they were before being archived. This helps verify historical information, review previous configurations, and reference the part’s state prior to archival. <br<br The page displays: <br  Part information (Part ID, Name, Status) <br  Linked operations or steps <br  Associated Machine  Uploaded documents <br  CNC Programs  Others|   How to Use Revisit   |  | || | You can review a part and see its details as they were before archival. <br Go to the Archive section and select the part. <br  Click Revisit icon to open the complete detail preview. <br  Review all displayed information for reference or historical context. <br  Use this view to understand the part’s previous state, including operations, metadata, and related records. |    Important Notes  |  | || |  Archiving is permanent unless restored by an authorized user. <br  Ensure that the part is no longer active or tied to ongoing processes. <br  Doublecheck dependencies or related modules before confirming. |    Revisit Overview How to Use Revisit Important Notes
